threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can catch a bus to central London without checking the TfL app first. The only constants here are delays and diversions.
Last week on the N159, a woman argued passionately with the driver about her lost shoe. The whole bus was silent until she declared it was a 'sacred relic'!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a hipster barista chatting with an old Turkish man over coffee, both laughing like old friends, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find ‘Späti’ culture, where corner shops become late-night hangouts, selling everything from beer to bike repairs at 3 AM.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can identify the exact BART delay by the shake of the ground. That Transbay Tube rumble? They’ve felt it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy using an old-school typewriter on the bus yesterday. Muni moves at snail's pace, but some still manage to make their own moments shine.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Festa Major is just a party, but it’s really about the neighbors reconnecting with each other, like an annual family reunion.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones staring at the metro map like it's an ancient scroll, trying to decode which line to take while missing the train that's right behind them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still pause at the bus stop when the 149 is late; locals have perfected the art of waiting without an ounce of impatience.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Adele. Strangers were singing along; felt like we were in a bizarre musical.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when the smell of grilled kebabs mixed with fresh coffee wafted through the street during the Sunday flea market.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, complete with art installations that shift every weekend.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their instinctive flinch when BART’s brakes screech in the Tube—it's not just the noise, it’s the PTSD from endless delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni rider serenading everyone with a ukulele this morning. Only in the TL would you find music mixed with the smell of lunch truck tacos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists miss how truly quiet Gràcia gets after 10 PM. They think it’s always a fiesta, but locals cherish the calm once the bars close.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they stop and stare at 90-degree intersections, trying to figure out which way is which. Locals just glide past.
